reason:
Both correctly diagnose Goodhart's law on MTTR and cover the core points: mean hides distribution, small-incident dilution, reclassification/gaming, and recommend SLO/error-budget burn, total impact-minutes, tail percentiles, and recurrence. B adds two genuinely new, decision-relevant points A lacks: MTTR clocks often starting at ticket-open rather than customer-impact-start (so slower detection hides behind a good MTTR), and a concrete recurrence metric definition plus change failure rate as a complementary signal. These would change what the asker instruments next, and B achieves this without added length via terser phrasing.</parameter>
</invoke>
